review_text: Spotify keeps recommending the same artists over and over. I've been trying to get out of my indie bubble for months but it just won't let me.
Discover Weekly used to be the reason I loved Spotify. Now it's just recycling songs I've already heard. What happened to the algorithm?
I find literally all my new music on Instagram Reels and then have to search for it on Spotify. The discovery feature is basically useless for me.
It's been 3 years and Spotify still recommends me the same 50 artists. I have 3000 liked songs but it refuses to go outside my comfort zone.
I played one Taylor Swift song at a party and now my recommendations are ruined for weeks. The algorithm has no memory or context.
Spotify's algorithm used to be much better. I suspect real human curators were involved before. Now it's clearly all automated and terrible.
Any EDM song I start a radio for leads to TikTok phonk and no matter how many times I skip, the same songs keep appearing.
I've been trying to discover jazz for months. Every playlist Spotify makes me is the same 20 jazz classics. There's a whole world out there.
I end up playing the same playlist every single day because everything Spotify suggests feels random and wrong. Comfort > risk.
My Liked Songs has 2000 tracks but I shuffle the same 30 songs. Spotify's UI makes it too hard to actually dig into your own library.
Discover Weekly doesn't know what mood I'm in. At 2am I want something atmospheric, not the upbeat pop it keeps throwing at me.
I had a specific mood — late night study vibes — and Spotify gave me workout music. The context awareness is basically zero.
Recommended songs for my chill playlist are 95% upbeat kpop. A genre I like but completely wrong for that playlist's vibe.
I believe my habit of digging deep cuts has tainted my recommendations. Now any popular song radio is filled with obscure 1-minute tracks.
The algorithm puts you in a bubble and actively fights you when you try to get out. It's like it punishes curiosity.
Played Christmas music in December. Still getting Christmas recommendations in March. The algorithm has no seasonal awareness.
I shared my account with my sister for one week. Now my taste profile is completely destroyed. There's no way to reset.
The suggested songs for my playlist filled with j-rock and ballads are 95% upbeat kpop songs. The algorithm has no logic.
My daily mixes are filled with songs I've already saved. I don't need my liked songs regurgitated — I need new music.
The recommendation system prioritizes songs already in my playlist instead of suggesting similar new tracks. This is backwards.
Last Friday my Release Radar didn't even update. I follow 47 artists and it showed me nothing new. What's happening?
Home page now shows loads of podcasts with no option to filter them out. I use Spotify for music, not podcasts.
I used to go on Spotify radio rabbit holes finding new music. It was so good at showing similar songs. Now it's completely broken.
Not interested button doesn't work. I've clicked it 6 times on the same horror podcast recommendation and it keeps appearing.
I wish there was a way to filter out songs less than 2 minutes long from recommendations. So much noise in my discovery.
Used to discover amazing music through Spotify. Now the algo just shows me the same 10 artists. Switching to Apple Music soon.
I'm paying for Premium but my Discover Weekly is the same songs every week. It's embarrassing how bad the recommendations have gotten.
2 stars. The app doesn't understand that I want different music at different times. Working music != gym music != late night music.
Algorithm is stuck. I've been listening to the same genre loop for 8 months. No matter what I try, it won't show me anything new.
Finding new music on Spotify feels like work now. I have to manually search for everything. The discovery is completely passive and wrong.
Discover Weekly introduced me to 5 artists this month that I absolutely love. This is why I pay for Premium. Keep it up!
The Daily Mix feature is genuinely great. It mixes my favorites with new stuff perfectly. Discovery feels natural not forced.
Good app overall but wish there was a mood filter for recommendations. Like tell it you're sad or energetic and it adjusts.
Found all my recent music on Instagram Reels. Spotify hasn't shown me anything new in months. 3 stars only because the player is good.
The algorithm is too safe. It never takes risks. I want to be surprised — instead I get the same comfortable recommendations forever.
Premium user for 4 years. Discovery used to be magical. Now I listen to the same 3 playlists on repeat because nothing new feels right.
Spotify used to be the best at finding new music. Now it's just a pretty player. Discovery is completely broken.
I want music that matches how I feel RIGHT NOW. Not what I listened to last week. Spotify doesn't understand real-time mood.
Been Premium for 5 years. The discovery peaked in 2019. Now I'm trapped in a genre bubble I can't escape no matter what I do.
The AI DJ feature actually surprised me this week with 3 songs I'd never heard that I now have on repeat. More of this please!
I hear songs on YouTube Shorts and by the time I search on Spotify I've forgotten the lyrics. Need a hum-to-search feature badly.
My partner says I listen to the same songs every morning. She's right. Not because I want to — because Spotify makes discovery hard.
Would love a Discovery Mode where I explicitly tell Spotify I want something completely new. Right now there's no control.
Played one sad song during a breakup. Spotify kept recommending sad music for 3 months straight. There's no way to reset the context.
I've tried everything — new playlists, different genres, explicit skip feedback. The algorithm just doesn't respond to my signals.
Loved Discover Weekly this week — it somehow knew I was going through a indie rock phase before I did. Genuinely magical when it works.
